title: Diagnostics, Build Reports, Metrics, Profiles, and Failure Triage · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded actionable build and publication evidence report and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.
Sort deterministically, deduplicate, cap cascades, promote configured warnings, summarize omissions, and return status from policy.
Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Diagnostic Model

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Redaction Retention
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-redaction-retention-1nzepj" that uses case 8673 to produce deterministic located diagnostics and bounded redacted build metrics. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-redaction-retention-1nzepj
# Competency: ssg-redaction-retention.
# Case 8673: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical.
def evidence_ssg_redaction_retention_1nzepj(diagnostics: int = 7, located: int = 7, build_stages: int = 10, measured_stages: int = 10, unredacted_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1nzepj = "1nzepj-jpezn1-8673"
    assert evidence_variant_1nzepj
    evidence_axes_1nzepj = (
        "reader1nzepj",
        "source8673",
        "parsejpezn1",
        "route1nzepj8673",
        "artifact86731nzepj",
        "recoveryjpezn18673",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1nzepj)) == 6
    if diagnostics < 0 or located < 0:
        return False, located, "diagnostic-count"
    if located != diagnostics:
        return False, located, "location-coverage"
    if build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages:
        return False, measured_stages, "metric-coverage"
    if unredacted_secrets > 0:
        return False, unredacted_secrets, "diagnostic-leak"
    assert located == diagnostics
    return True, measured_stages, "actionable-build-report"
